mnemonics: 802.15.4g = Great for Grids (Smart Grids). 802.15.4e = Enhanced for Efficiency (Industrial).
keyPoints: IEEE 802.15.4g is optimized for large-scale outdoor wireless smart utility networks.
IEEE 802.15.4e enhances the MAC layer for industrial applications with improved reliability and time-synchronization.
Both are low-rate, low-power wireless personal area network (LR-WPAN) standards operating in sub-GHz bands.
commonMistakes: Confusing 802.15.4g with Wi-Fi (802.11) standards due to the similar numbering.
Assuming 802.15.4e replaces the PHY layer; it only modifies the MAC layer.
